markdown.headingText: Browse the atlas as a living map.
markdown.markdown:
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: atlas-landing-page :::::::::: {.essays-hero .atlas-hero} ::::::::: {.container .py-5} :::::::: {.row .align-items-end} ::::: col-lg-8 ::: essays-kicker Thinking Across Scales ::: ::: essays-tagline Places that shaped the way I think. ::: ::::: :::: col-lg-4 ::: atlas-hero-intro A growing atlas of landscapes, field observations, and journeys that changed the way I see ecological systems. ::: :::: :::::::: ::::::::: :::::::::: ::::::::::::: {.atlas-section .atlas-section-space .py-5 .py-lg-6} :::::::::::: container ::::::: {.row .align-items-end .mb-4} :::: col-lg-7 ::: essays-section-label Thinking Through Space ::: :::: :::: col-lg-5 ::: {.atlas-section-intro .mb-0} Geography is not just backdrop here. Each landscape opened a different way of seeing pattern, scale, and ecological relation. ::: :::: ::::::: :::::: atlas-map-shell
::: atlas-map-caption Hover across the markers to preview a place. Click to read the full journey. ::: :::::: :::::::::::: ::::::::::::: :::::::::::::: {.atlas-section .atlas-section-time .py-5 .py-lg-6} ::::::::::::: container ::::::: {.row .align-items-end .mb-4} :::: col-lg-7 ::: essays-section-label Thinking Through Time ::: ## Follow how the thinking evolved. {#atlas-time-heading .mb-0} :::: :::: col-lg-5 ::: {.atlas-section-intro .mb-0} This timeline is another route through the atlas: a way of tracing how one place led to another, and how observation slowly became method. ::: :::: ::::::: ::::::: atlas-timeline-shell :::: atlas-timeline-header ::: {.atlas-timeline-instruction .mb-0} Drag across the years to move through the atlas. :::

:::: :::: atlas-timeline-rail ::: {#atlas-timeline-stops .atlas-timeline-stops} ::: ::::
::::::: ::::::::::::: :::::::::::::: :::::::::: {.atlas-section .atlas-section-featured .py-5 .py-lg-6} ::::::::: container :::::::: atlas-feature-shell ::: {.atlas-feature-photo .atlas-photo .atlas-photo--everglades role="img" aria-label="Editorial landscape study of the Everglades"} ::: :::::: atlas-feature-copy ::: essays-section-label Editor's Pick ::: ## Where Scale Became Visible {#atlas-featured-heading} ::: atlas-feature-meta Everglades · Florida, United States · 2009 ::: ::: atlas-feature-excerpt In the Everglades, water, fire, and slow ecological time stopped feeling like separate subjects. They became a single system—one that taught me to read landscapes not as scenery, but as layered processes moving at different speeds. ::: [Read Story →](./everglades.html){.essay-card-link} :::::: :::::::: ::::::::: :::::::::: ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
